task_completion.visual_evidence: The YDBGui application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Locate a patient's rated disabilities in the ^DPT global
task_completion.visual_evidence: YDBGui is displaying the ^DPT global, specifically showing a patient record expanded to reveal node .372 (Rated Disabilities).

task_completion.description: Cross-reference the disability name in the ^DIC(31) global
task_completion.visual_evidence: YDBGui is displaying the ^DIC(31) global, showing the lookup of the specific disability code found in the patient's record to get the human-readable disability name.

task_completion.description: Open or create the required text report file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano, vim) is open, and the file path /home/ga/Documents/disability_audit.txt is visible in the window title, status bar, or save dialog.

task_completion.description: Write the patient disability details in the exact required format
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor contains the exact format: 'Patient: [Name]', 'Disability: [Name]', and 'Percentage: [Number]%' using the data retrieved from YDBGui.

integrity.description: The agent used the YDBGui interface to explore the database, rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the graphical YDBGui window to view globals, not terminal commands like 'mumps -dir' or direct database queries via CLI.

integrity.description: The data entered into the report matches the data viewed in the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Patient Name, Disability Name, and Percentage in the text file should correspond exactly to the values seen in the YDBGui ^DPT and ^DIC(31) screens, proving the agent didn't hallucinate or copy-paste a pre-existing answer.

integrity.description: The agent actually created and saved the file in a text editor or via standard file writing commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Evidence of a text editor being used or a terminal command (like echo or cat) writing to the specific file path, rather than just printing the text to the console.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file must be exactly at '/home/ga/Documents/disability_audit.txt'. The patient data is located in the '^DPT' global under node '.372'. The human-readable disability names are cross-referenced in the '^DIC(31)' global.
